title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. magonica
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. magonica, also known as Magenta Gray Wormwood, is a perennial shrub belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial, antifungal, and insecticidal properties, which make it effective in treating skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. magonica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and boost the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
